item.title: The Dance Legend Lucinda Childs’s “Momentary Reprise”
item.description: Also: the images of Yves Saint Laurent, “Girl, Interrupted” reviewed, the fusionist wonderland of Tortoise, and more.

item.title: What Marcel Is Selling
item.description: At the new restaurant in the Sotheby’s-owned Breuer building, money, in its most indiscreet sense, is everywhere.
item.dc:creator: ByHelen Rosner

item.title: The Difference Between the Knicks and the White House Cage Fight
item.description: Sports, spectacle, and what Juvenal would have made of this moment.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Gopnik

item.title: Pencils Up! The Knicks on Broadway
item.description: The N.B.A. championship was a win for Mayor Mamdani, but the city’s public-school kids, stuck taking their Regents exams as the ticker-tape parade thundered past their windows, weren’t so sure.
item.dc:creator: ByDan Greene

item.title: Alexandra Grant Brings Spirit Back
item.description: Walking through her new exhibition, “Antigone 3000,” the artist known to online hordes as Keanu Reeves’s mysterious silver-haired girlfriend reflects on Sophocles and the color pink.
item.dc:creator: BySarah Larson

item.title: Dan Mintz, Reanimated
item.description: The comedian and voice artist puts his “Bob’s Burgers” expertise to the test with a cartoon standup special—produced by the man who officiated his wedding, John Mulaney.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Allen

item.title: Isabel J. Kim Makes Her Own World
item.description: At a board-game café on the Upper West Side, the lawyer and author discusses her new book, “Sublimation,” about borders, parallel selves, and an eerily Trump-like government.
item.dc:creator: ByLuis A. Gómez

item.title: The Teen Believers in a Christian America
item.description: For Charlie Kirk’s followers, faith and patriotism are intertwined.
item.dc:creator: ByEliza Griswold

item.title: What Science Knows About Grief
item.description: After my husband’s death, I had never been more pliable, tender, open, or raw. It was then that I tried E.M.D.R. therapy.
item.dc:creator: ByAmanda Petrusich

item.title: The Repo Man Coming for Your Ride
item.description: As America’s auto debt nears $1.7 trillion, repossessions are reaching levels not seen since the Great Recession. Inside an industry at the front line of the country’s affordability crisis.
item.dc:creator: ByPaige Williams

item.title: Colson Whitehead’s Big Score
item.description: As he closes out his Harlem crime trilogy with “Cool Machine,” the two-time Pulitzer winner turns again to the city that made him, and to the private ghosts behind his restless reinventions.
item.dc:creator: ByJulian Lucas

item.title: The Pied Piper
item.description: The man with the fife was good at getting rid of Hamelin’s rats. What, the townspeople wondered, could he do with the children?
item.dc:creator: BySimon Rich

item.title: “The Readers”
item.description: It would be one thing if I wrote fiction about Cromwell or aliens, but, given that my protagonists resemble me, how could I know you weren’t mixing us up?
item.dc:creator: ByBen Lerner

item.title: Why the Odyssey Keeps Defeating Filmmakers
item.description: Full of violence, desire, monsters, and magic, Homer’s epic has tempted directors for decades. Can Christopher Nolan’s new adaptation survive the voyage?
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Denby

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “A Terrible Intimacy,” “This Is Not About Running,” “The Summer Boy,” and “The Children.”

item.title: What’s the Point of Sex, Anyway?
item.description: The world’s life-forms reproduce sexually in a bewildering variety of ways, even though scientists still aren’t sure why they bother.
item.dc:creator: ByElizabeth Kolbert

item.title: The Curious Career of “the American Dream”
item.description: How a phrase coined during the Depression became a national creed, a global brand, and a vessel for disillusionment.
item.dc:creator: ByHua Hsu

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo’s Early-Twenties Lament
item.description: On her new album, “You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love,” the singer inches away from frisky pop-punk and toward the velvety yearning of New Wave.
item.dc:creator: ByAmanda Petrusich

item.title: “The Invite” Is a Witty Relationship Comedy That Could Be Wilder
item.description: In Olivia Wilde’s bickersome couples comedy, an evening of refreshments and recriminations leads to an intriguing proposition.
item.dc:creator: ByJustin Chang

item.title: “On Form”
item.description: “I am trying to write an essay, I said, about form.”
item.dc:creator: ByLinda Gregerson

item.title: “Domesticity”
item.description: “The owls weren’t in distress, although / I thought they sounded like it.”
item.dc:creator: ByLindsay Turner

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the June 29, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Wednesday, June 17, 2026
item.description: A beginner-friendly puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByRobyn Weintraub
